title: Define the minimal canonical continuation contract in code
plan_sections: Server-owned transaction variables vs client-managed inputs
goal: Define and enforce the minimal canonical continuation contract in code so deterministic resume depends only on the top-level canonical fields identified in the plan.
why_this_ticket: The plan defines a deliberately small continuation surface. This ticket makes that contract explicit in the runtime code before broader recovery and identity changes are implemented.
scope: Identify the code paths and data structures that represent the materialized continuation state.
Define the canonical top-level continuation fields required for deterministic resume.
Ensure the continuation contract distinguishes active-transaction state from the no-active baseline.
Ensure lifecycle classification remains top-level via status and is not duplicated as an authoritative field elsewhere.
Document any currently present fields that should be treated as non-canonical, derived, or optional.
out_of_scope: Implementing compatibility normalization for historical logs.
Adding or removing regression tests beyond what is needed to validate the contract shape.
Making session context part of the continuation contract.
expected_outputs: A clear code-level definition of the minimal continuation contract.
A list of runtime structures or modules that own the continuation contract.
A mapping from plan-defined canonical continuation fields to concrete code locations.
A short note identifying fields that are currently present but must not control deterministic continuation.
A gap note identifying remaining helper/runtime dependencies that still rely on non-canonical active_tx metadata and therefore block terminal completion of this ticket.

no_active_baseline_requirements.last_applied_seq: retains the last materialized event sequence when available

rules: Treat the listed canonical_fields as the only top-level continuation fields required for deterministic resume.
Treat status as the canonical lifecycle classification field and keep it top-level.
Treat active_tx as an identity-bearing active-transaction snapshot, not as the lifecycle-classification field.
Do not promote phase-style, step-style, session-derived, or guidance-only fields into the continuation contract.
Do not redefine canonical continuation behavior through helper-style API labels.
acceptance_criteria: The code-level continuation contract contains exactly the canonical fields identified in the plan for deterministic resume.
The no-active baseline is represented structurally and matches the plan.
The implementation clearly distinguishes active_tx from top-level status responsibilities.
Optional or derived metadata is not required for continuation correctness.
The resulting contract can be described in terms of the minimal canonical continuation contract plus top-level next_action.
Helper and runtime continuation paths no longer require active_tx phase/current_step/session metadata as correctness-critical inputs.
notes: This ticket should be completed before tickets that depend on exact active-transaction continuation behavior.
Any ambiguity found while mapping fields should be resolved in favor of the minimal contract defined in plan.md.
Corrective plan: restrict next_action derivation to top-level canonical fields, remove canonical sentinel no-active semantics, and reduce active_tx consumers to identity-only continuation use.
Follow-up boundary: helper execution flows that still depend on active_tx phase/current_step/session metadata remain out of scope for p1-t02 and should be handled by later tickets.

Current audit result: the materialized contract and workflow guidance were narrowed toward the minimal canonical surface, but repo/commit helper paths still depend on active_tx phase/current_step/session metadata and therefore acceptance remains incomplete.
Current audit result: tickets_list.json bookkeeping must not mark p1-t02 as done until the remaining helper/runtime dependencies are removed or the ticket boundary is explicitly re-scoped.

Concrete remaining dependency: src/agentops_mcp_server/repo_tools.py::_load_tx_context still requires active_tx.session_id and still derives phase/step execution context from active_tx.phase and active_tx.current_step.

Concrete remaining dependency: src/agentops_mcp_server/repo_tools.py::_emit_tx_event still writes phase/current_step/session_id back into materialized active_tx before appending tx events, which keeps helper continuation coupled to non-canonical active_tx metadata.

Concrete remaining dependency: src/agentops_mcp_server/commit_manager.py::_load_tx_context still requires active_tx.session_id and still derives commit execution context from active_tx.phase and active_tx.current_step.

Concrete remaining dependency: src/agentops_mcp_server/commit_manager.py::_emit_tx_begin_with_context and _ensure_tx_begin still repopulate active_tx.phase/current_step/session_id into materialized state, so helper bootstrap still assumes old-shaped active_tx metadata.

Concrete remaining dependency: src/agentops_mcp_server/state_store.py::tx_event_append_and_state_save still materializes phase/current_step/session_id and other helper-oriented fields under active_tx, so canonical-state writes are not yet reduced to an identity-only active_tx snapshot.

Concrete remaining dependency: src/agentops_mcp_server/state_store.py::_current_active_tx_context still reports status/phase/current_step/next_action/session_id from active_tx, which shows diagnostics and helper context are still modeled around old-shaped active_tx content.

Concrete remaining dependency: tests/test_repo_tools.py and tests/test_commit_manager.py still build and assert old-shaped active_tx fixtures containing status/phase/current_step/session_id/next_action nested under active_tx, so regression coverage still encodes the pre-minimal continuation contract.
Follow-on ticket mapping: move active_tx write-shape reduction in src/agentops_mcp_server/state_store.py and related materialized-state cleanup into p2-t04.
Follow-on ticket mapping: move canonical next_action-driven continuation cleanup in src/agentops_mcp_server/repo_tools.py and src/agentops_mcp_server/commit_manager.py into p2-t05.
Follow-on ticket mapping: move session_id de-hardening so helper continuation no longer treats session context as correctness-critical into p2-t07.

Follow-on ticket mapping: move old-shaped helper regression fixture cleanup in tests/test_repo_tools.py and tests/test_commit_manager.py alongside the corresponding p2 runtime changes rather than forcing partial updates in p1-t02.

Concrete remaining dependency: src/agentops_mcp_server/repo_tools.py::_load_tx_context still requires active_tx.session_id and still derives phase/step execution context from active_tx.phase and active_tx.current_step.

Concrete remaining dependency: src/agentops_mcp_server/repo_tools.py::_emit_tx_event still writes phase/current_step/session_id back into materialized active_tx before appending tx events, which keeps helper continuation coupled to non-canonical active_tx metadata.

Concrete remaining dependency: src/agentops_mcp_server/commit_manager.py::_load_tx_context still requires active_tx.session_id and still derives commit execution context from active_tx.phase and active_tx.current_step.
